definition: Aggregate microscopy-derived morphology features, CellProfiler profiles, or learned image embeddings for wells, compounds, genes, or perturbations, then compare profiles with cosine, Pearson correlation, or a learned phenotypic distance. For SPECTRA, this audits whether model performance depends on evaluation perturbations having morphology profiles close to training perturbations.
example_usage: python embedding_cosine_similarity.py --train train_morphology.csv --eval eval_morphology.csv --out pairwise_similarity.csv --embedding-cols-prefix morph_

citations.title: Three million images and morphological profiles of cells treated with matched chemical and genetic perturbations

evidence.summary: The paper frames image-based profiles as a way to identify genetic and chemical perturbations with similar impacts on cell morphology.
The supplementary material reports cosine similarities between compound and genetic perturbation profiles.
The paper discusses quantitative similarity of cell images and image-based profiles for perturbation impact.

limitations: Morphology profiles can be dominated by plate, site, cell line, dose, time point, segmentation, and normalization choices.
Profile similarity may reflect toxicity or cell-cycle state rather than the mechanism of interest.
Single-cell and well-level aggregation can produce different similarity geometries.
quality_gates: Report assay, cell line, time point, dose, feature extraction method, aggregation, and normalization.
Run batch/site/cell-line curves alongside morphology-similarity curves.
Check replicate retrieval or positive-control enrichment before using the axis for model auditing.
